intro: Curated for Nick: workplace-personalized-agent thesis (memory that doesn't drift, self-assembling agents, governance/verification, knowledge graphs). Your pass = Expo Explorer + Overflow, so Expo-Stage talks are in-person; Main-Stage keynotes are overflow viewing; a few breakouts are flagged chase = catch it if your pass gets you in, otherwise the recording.

picks.title: From Context to Memory: Your Agents Need a Real Memory Layer
picks.why: Your north star said out loud. This is the memory-layer problem you've been solving, on an Expo Stage you can walk into.

picks.title: The Infinite Context Window Is a Myth: Context Engineering for AI Agents
picks.why: Context engineering = your 'always has the right context, day or month later.' In-person on an Expo Stage.

picks.title: In Code They Act, In Proof We Trust
picks.why: Verification/proof for agent actions = your governance layer. Erik Meijer is a legend (LINQ, Rx) — worth the overflow seat.

picks.title: In the Land of AI Agents, the Verifiers Are King
picks.why: Verifiers = your 'every claim checked against the source before it's stated.' Tariq is CEO of Sonar.

picks.title: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of Separating the Task from the Model
picks.why: Orchestration over raw models = exactly how you build (direct the system, don't type the code). From the DSPy crew.

picks.title: CrabRAG: Why Automated Assistants Need Graph Memory, Not More Tokens
picks.why: Graph memory = your brain layer. Speaker is Stephen Chin (Neo4j) — see the People list, worth finding him after.

picks.title: Citation Needed: Provenance for LLM-Built Knowledge Graphs
picks.why: Provenance for knowledge graphs = your verification + brain combined. Speaker is Daniel Chalef (Zep AI) — your #1 person to meet.

picks.title: Total Recall: Agent Memory and Harness Engineering
picks.why: Agent memory + the harness around it. Day-1 workshop; closest thing to a deep-dive on your core problem.

picks.title: Model Whisperers: How Evals and Prompts Shape Agent Behavior
picks.why: Evals + prompts shaping behavior = the tuning loop behind your self-assembling layer.

people.name: Daniel Chalef
people.title: Founder & CEO, Zep AI
people.why: Zep productizes agent memory — your exact problem, as a company. The single most useful person here for you.
people.hook: How do you keep memory correct across sessions, not just retrievable? Continuity is the wall I keep hitting building my own system.

people.name: Stephen Chin
people.title: VP DevRel, Neo4j
people.why: Graph memory for agents (his CrabRAG talk) — the brain layer you built, productized on a graph DB.
people.hook: When does graph memory beat just stuffing more tokens? I'm running a knowledge graph over my own work and want to know where it breaks.

people.name: Laurie Voss
people.title: Head of DevRel, Arize AI
people.why: Evals + observability for shipping agents = your governance layer. Also ex-npm (seldo), extremely well-connected.
people.hook: How do teams actually catch when an agent's state has drifted — before it acts on the wrong context?

people.name: Anders Swanson
people.title: Developer Evangelist, Oracle
people.why: His talk is literally 'your agents need a real memory layer.' Direct trade-notes opportunity.
people.hook: What does a real memory layer look like at Oracle scale vs. a single-user system like mine?

people.name: Morgan Willis
people.title: AWS
people.why: Context engineering for agents — the 'infinite context is a myth' angle that matches how you think about memory.
people.hook: Where does context engineering stop being enough and you actually need persistent memory?

people.name: Erik Meijer
people.title: Research Scholar, Leibniz Labs
people.why: Verification/proof for agent actions = your governance layer, from someone who shaped LINQ and Rx. Listen more than talk.
people.hook: Ask about proof-carrying agent actions — how do you make an autonomous system's outputs trustworthy by construction?

neilNote: Neil McKechnie / Credegra is not a listed speaker or sponsor at the Fair, so he won't show up in this app. Coordinate in person via the LinkedIn message in your pipeline notes — if he has a booth he'll tell you where to find him.
